hero.title: One shared place for your child — across homes
hero.description: You should not have to be the one who always remembers. Lina keeps the practical details — medical info, equipment, contacts, schedules — shared and up to date across both homes.

csSection.label: Care Schedule
csSection.title: Plan your care schedule together
csSection.description: Lina's interactive care schedule lets both parents plan and visualize how time is shared across weeks. See day splits, weekend patterns, and handover frequency — all in one clear view.

caSection.label: Care Agreement
caSection.title: What should the agreement include?
caSection.description: A good care agreement gives predictability for the child and both parents. Read about what the agreement should cover, see an example, and find links to official resources.

how.label: How it works
how.title: Set it up once. The rest is shared automatically.

step1.title: Create a shared space
step1.description: One parent sets up a space for the child. The other gets an invite to join. No complicated setup required.

step2.title: Add what matters
step2.description: Medical information, allergies, contacts, equipment lists — add it once, and it is available in both homes.

step3.title: No one needs to ask twice
step3.description: Updates are visible to both parents in real time. No more repeating yourself, or wondering if the other home has the latest.

features.label: Features
features.title: All the details, shared with the other home

threads.title: Threads
threads.description: Separate conversations for each topic — logistics, school, health. Nothing gets buried in one long chat.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Messages cannot be edited or deleted. Search conversations, mark what matters, and always have a clear record of what was agreed.

album.title: Shared album
album.description: A private album where both parents can share photos. The moments that happen in one home are visible in both.

features.more: Also included: contacts, equipment lists, child information, and care schedule — so both parents have what they need, wherever the child is.

contacts.title: Shared contacts
contacts.description: Pediatrician, teacher, after-school program — important contacts in one place, always up to date.

equipment.title: Equipment lists
equipment.description: Rain boots, gym bag, allergy medicine — shared lists make it easier to keep track of what needs to follow the child between homes.

childinfo.title: Child information
childinfo.description: Birth date, allergies, medication, shoe size — all the details that matter, gathered in one place and always accessible.

schedule.title: Care schedule
schedule.description: Create a visual overview of how time is shared between homes. See the full schedule at a glance, week by week.

control.title: Built for difficult days too
control.description: Export all messages for documentation. Blur photos to reduce emotional weight. Save the images you want to keep. Control what you see and when.

problem.label: Why Lina
problem.title: When care is split across two homes, details get lost
problem.description: When plans, messages and photos live in different apps, important things fall through the cracks. Lina brings everything into one shared structure — calm, clear and predictable.

forWhom.forDescription: Parents who share care across two homes and want less friction in everyday cooperation. For those who value structure over noise and clarity over constant messaging.
forWhom.notDescription: Not a social network, not a general family chat, and not built for endless conversation. Lina is a focused tool designed specifically for cooperation around your child.

privacy.label: Privacy
privacy.title: Private by design
privacy.description: What happens in Lina stays between the two of you. Your child's information is private — and no one outside has access to what you share.

point1.title: No advertising
point1.description: Your experience is never shaped by ads or commercial interests.

point2.title: No data sharing
point2.description: Your data stays yours. It is never shared with third parties.

point3.title: No algorithm
point3.description: You decide what you see — not an algorithm optimizing for engagement.

privacy.tagline: Structured for cooperation. Private by default.

cta.title: One safe line between two parents.
cta.description: Lina brings everything your child needs into one shared, organized space. Download and get started in minutes.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: One subscription. Both parents.
pricing.description: Lina+ gives both parents full access with a single subscription. Start with a free trial — no payment required until you decide.

free.name: Free
free.f2: Up to 100 photos in shared album

pricing.whyText1: Lina has no advertising and does not share data with third parties. The subscription is what makes that possible — it funds development, hosting, and support without compromising your privacy.
pricing.whyText2: Both parents get full access with one subscription. The parent who subscribes covers both — so the other parent never has to pay.

p1.title: One subscription, two parents
p1.description: Both parents get full access. No extra cost for the other parent.

p2.title: No advertising, no data sharing
p2.description: The subscription funds the app. Your data is never sold.

p3.title: No commitment
p3.description: Cancel anytime in App Store or Google Play.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.intro: Have a question about Lina or need help with your account? We're here to help — send us a message and we'll get back to you as soon as we can.
contact.successDescription: We'll get back to you as soon as we can.

cs.heading: Care Schedule
cs.subhead: See how time is divided between homes — week by week.
cs.intro: Click on the days to assign them to a parent. The split updates automatically, and you can export the plan as a PDF.
cs.step2: Click a day to toggle between parent A, parent B, or unassigned
cs.step3: See the split in the summary below the calendar
cs.exportDesc: Print or save the schedule as a PDF.

hero.label: For professionals
hero.title: A tool you can recommend to parents who share care
hero.description: Lina helps parents keep the practical details of everyday co-parenting organized and accessible across two homes. This page is intended for professionals.

about.label: About the app
about.title: One shared place for the child — across homes
about.description: Many parents who share care lack a common structure for everyday cooperation. Information is scattered across texts, emails, and verbal agreements, and one parent ends up coordinating everything. Lina brings the practical details — medical information, contacts, equipment lists, photos, and messages — into one shared space that both parents can access.

when.label: When Lina is a good fit

fits.title: Lina works well when parents
fits.item1: have a fundamentally workable co-parenting relationship but struggle with the practical coordination
fits.item2: want more structure and predictability in their everyday routines
fits.item3: need a shared communication channel that keeps cooperation focused
fits.item4: want to ensure important information about the child is available in both homes

notFits.title: Lina is not suitable when
notFits.item1: there is high conflict requiring legal or therapeutic intervention
notFits.item2: one parent is unwilling to engage in digital cooperation
notFits.item3: the situation requires measures beyond practical coordination

privacy.label: Privacy
privacy.title: Secure and private by design
privacy.description: Lina is built with privacy as its foundation. No advertising, no data sharing, and no algorithm influencing what users see. As a professional, you can recommend the app with confidence that parents' information is handled responsibly.

point1.title: No advertising
point1.description: No ads or commercial interests affect the user experience.

point2.title: No data sharing
point2.description: Parents' data is not sold or shared with third parties.

point3.title: Secure infrastructure
point3.description: Information is protected through secure storage, access control, and signed links.

features.label: How it works in practice
features.title: Structure that reduces friction in everyday life

threads.title: Threads
threads.description: Parents communicate about one topic at a time — logistics, school, or health — without important information getting lost in a long message thread.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Messages cannot be edited or deleted. This creates a predictable communication channel and a clear record of what has been agreed.

album.title: Shared album
album.description: Both parents can share photos from everyday life. The child is visible in both homes.

info.title: Child information
info.description: Medical information, allergies, and important details are entered once and available to both parents — regardless of which home the child is in.

equipment.title: Equipment lists
equipment.description: A clear overview of what the child needs in each home reduces the number of clarifications and misunderstandings around practical details.

schedule.title: Care schedule
schedule.description: Both parents see the care arrangement in the same view. Fewer discussions about who had the child and when.

agreement.title: Care Agreement
agreement.description: The care agreement page gives parents an overview of what the agreement should include, with an example and links to official resources. A good starting point before or after mediation.

ca.pageTitle: Care Agreement — what should it include? | Lina
ca.pageDesc: Learn what a good care agreement should cover. See an example, get tips for the process, and find links to official resources.
ca.label: Parenting Agreement
ca.heroTitle: What should a parenting agreement include?
ca.heroDesc: A parenting agreement helps parents create predictability for the child when care is shared across two homes. Here you will find an overview of what the agreement should cover, an example, and links to official resources.
ca.aboutTitle: An agreement that grows with the child
ca.aboutDesc: A parenting agreement is a voluntary, written arrangement between parents describing how care for the child is organised across two homes. It is not legally binding in the same way as a court order, but gives the family a shared framework to follow. A good agreement reflects the child's everyday life — and should be adjusted as the child grows.
ca.point1Title: Not legally binding like a court order
ca.point1Desc: The agreement is private and based on mutual consent between the parents. If you cannot agree, a family mediation service can help.
ca.point2Title: The child's best interests at the centre
ca.point2Desc: The agreement should be based on the child's needs, not the parents'. Children who are old enough should be heard and given the opportunity to express their views.
ca.point3Desc: A verbal agreement is hard to follow up on and easy to misunderstand. A written agreement provides clarity for both parents and can be used as a reference in case of disagreement.
ca.point4Desc: A child's needs change with age. A good parenting agreement is reviewed and adjusted regularly — ideally once a year or when circumstances change significantly.
ca.infoNote: This page provides general information and does not replace legal advice. If you cannot agree, we recommend contacting your local family services.
ca.coverTitle: Points a good parenting agreement should cover
ca.coverIntro: Not all points are relevant for everyone. Include what suits your situation.
ca.cover1Desc: How are the weeks divided? A consistent pattern — for example alternating weeks or a 3-4-4-3 arrangement — gives the child a clear rhythm.
ca.cover2Desc: Does the child have a primary residence with one parent, or shared residence? The residence affects rights and responsibilities.
ca.cover3Desc: Agree on the time and place for handovers. Clear arrangements reduce misunderstandings and give the child a calm transition.
ca.cover4Desc: The allocation of Christmas, Easter, summer holiday, the child's birthday, and other days off should be made explicit.
ca.cover5Desc: How expenses for activities, clothing, equipment, and other needs are shared. Clear agreements prevent disputes.
ca.cover6Desc: How do parents communicate about the child day to day? Agree on the channel, response time, and what is important to report.
ca.cover7Desc: Arrangements for travel abroad: notice period, consent, and where passports and other important documents are kept.
ca.cover8Desc: How are changes to the agreement handled? Agree on a regular review frequency — ideally once a year.
ca.exampleTitle: What a parenting agreement can look like
ca.exampleIntro: Below you can see an example of how a completed parenting agreement might look. Use this as inspiration when creating your own.
ca.tmplMeta: James Smith and Sarah Smith · Created 15 March 2026
ca.tmplResidence: Shared residence. Both parents have equal day-to-day decision-making authority.
ca.tmplSchedule: Alternating weeks, from Monday morning to the following Monday morning. The arrangement takes effect 1 September 2026.
ca.tmplHandover: Handovers take place via nursery or school. The parent beginning their week drops Emma off in the morning and assumes parental responsibility from that point. On days when nursery/school is closed, handover takes place at 4:00 pm at the home of the parent whose week has ended.
ca.tmplXmasDesc: The Christmas holiday is divided into two periods: period 1 (23–27 December) and period 2 (28 December – 1 January). Parents alternate between periods each year.
ca.tmplXmasA: Parent A has period 1 in even-numbered years and period 2 in odd-numbered years.
ca.tmplXmasB: Parent B has period 1 in odd-numbered years and period 2 in even-numbered years.
ca.tmplXmasHandover: Handover between periods takes place at 12:00 noon.
ca.tmplXmasDeadline: Allocation is confirmed in writing by 1 November.
ca.tmplEasterDesc: The Easter holiday runs from the Wednesday before Good Friday through to Easter Monday. Parents alternate each year.
ca.tmplEasterAlt: Parent A has even-numbered years, Parent B has odd-numbered years.
ca.tmplEasterDeadline: Allocation is confirmed in writing by 1 February.
ca.tmplSummerDesc: Each parent may have up to three consecutive weeks of holiday with Emma. Who chooses first alternates each year.
ca.tmplSummerAlt: Parent A chooses first in odd-numbered years, Parent B in even-numbered years.
ca.tmplSummerDeadline: Allocation is confirmed in writing by 1 April.
ca.tmplNationalDesc: Spent with each parent on alternating years.
ca.tmplNationalAlt: Parent A has odd-numbered years, Parent B has even-numbered years.
ca.tmplOtherHolDesc: For public holidays not specifically regulated, the regular arrangement applies. If such a day falls on a handover day, the handover moves to 12:00 noon unless otherwise agreed in writing.
ca.tmplBirthdayDesc: Emma's birthday is celebrated with each parent on alternating years. The hosting parent is responsible for organising and covering costs. Close family from both sides may be invited.
ca.tmplBirthdayAlt: Parent A organises in even-numbered years, Parent B in odd-numbered years.
ca.tmplContactDesc: While Emma is with one parent, she may have phone or video contact with the other 1–2 times per week. The timing is adapted to Emma's routines and mood. Calls are kept brief and calm.
ca.tmplSwapDesc: Changes may be made when both parents agree and it is practically feasible. Predictability is prioritised — frequent swaps are avoided. If there is disagreement, the regular arrangement applies.
ca.tmplComm: Parents communicate in writing about practical matters relating to Emma. The tone should be factual and respectful. Neither parent should speak negatively about the other in Emma's presence.
ca.tmplTravel: Foreign travel with Emma must be notified in writing at least four weeks in advance. Passports are kept with the parent at whose address Emma is registered.
ca.tmplAddressDesc: Emma is registered at Parent A's address. Changing the registered address requires written consent from both parents. In case of disagreement, both parties commit to mediation before any change is made. School placement is discussed no later than one year before school start.
ca.tmplHealthDesc: Both parents must be kept informed of appointments and assessments relating to Emma's health, development, and follow-up from a doctor, health visitor, or nursery/school. Both have the right to attend consultations and meetings.
ca.tmplFamilyDesc: Emma should have the opportunity to build secure relationships with both parents' families and close networks. Both parents should actively facilitate this.
ca.tmplFinanceDesc: Ongoing expenses for Emma are covered from a shared account to which both parents contribute an agreed monthly amount. Child benefit is transferred to the same account. Larger or unexpected expenses are agreed in advance. Both parents have full access to the account.
ca.tmplChangesDesc: This agreement replaces all previous verbal and written agreements between the parents. Changes are only valid when made in writing and signed by both parties. The agreement is reviewed at least every six months to ensure it continues to meet Emma's needs.
ca.tmplDisclaimer: This is an example. Adapt the content to your own situation.
ca.resourcesTitle: Make your agreement with official tools
ca.resourcesIntro: Several countries offer free tools and guidance for parents making a care agreement. We recommend using the official resources in your country.
ca.res1Desc: Bufdir's co-parenting agreement tool is a free, digital service where parents complete the agreement step by step and sign with BankID.
ca.res2Desc: In Sweden, parents can get help from the municipality's family law office to write an agreement on custody, residence, and contact. The agreement is approved by the social welfare committee.
ca.res3Desc: Familieretshuset offers a form for a parenting agreement that parents can complete themselves. The agreement is binding once both have signed.
ca.res4Desc: In Finland, agreements on child custody and care are drawn up with the municipality's child welfare supervisor (lastenvalvoja). The agreement is confirmed and becomes legally binding.
ca.promoDesc: Once the parenting agreement is in place, everyday life begins. Lina helps parents with the rest: messages, child information, photos, and equipment lists — all in one shared place across two homes.
ca.csPromoTitle: Plan the schedule week by week
ca.csPromoDesc: Once the agreement is in place, Lina's interactive care schedule helps you plan and visualize day-to-day life. See day splits, weekend patterns, and handover frequency — all in one clear view.

faq.pageDesc: Answers to common questions about Lina — the co-parenting app. Find answers about privacy, account, care schedule, and care agreement.
faq.heroDesc: Find answers to the most common questions about Lina, the care schedule, and the care agreement.
faq.cat1a1: Lina is an app for parents who share care. It brings together practical details — child information, messages, photos, contacts, and equipment lists — in one shared place accessible to both homes.
faq.cat1a2: Lina is made for parents who share care of a child between two homes. The app works best when the basic co-parenting relationship is functional, but more structure and predictability are needed.
faq.cat1a3: Yes, Lina is free to download and use. No payment is required to create an account or invite the other parent.
faq.cat1a4: Lina is available as an app for iPhone and Android. The care schedule at getlina.app can be used in a browser without downloading the app.
faq.cat1q5: Do both parents need to use the app?
faq.cat1a5: To use shared features like messages, photos, and child information, both parents need an account. The care schedule can be used by one parent alone.
faq.cat2q1: What is the care schedule in Lina?
faq.cat2a1: The care schedule is a free, browser-based tool where parents can plan and visualize how time is shared across weeks. You can see day splits, weekend patterns, and handover frequency in one clear view.
faq.cat2q2: Do I need an account to use the care schedule?
faq.cat2a2: No, the care schedule can be used without an account directly in a browser. You only need to download the app to use shared features like messages and child information.
faq.cat2q3: Can I export the care schedule?
faq.cat2a3: Yes, you can export the care schedule as a PDF directly from the tool. The PDF includes a visual overview of the split and can be used as an attachment to a care agreement.
faq.cat2q4: Can I plan holidays and special periods in the care schedule?
faq.cat2a4: Yes, the care schedule supports flexible week-by-week planning, making it easy to add holiday weeks and special periods.
faq.cat3q1: Who can see the child's information?
faq.cat3a1: Only the two parents connected to the child in Lina have access to the child's information. No other users or Lina itself has access to the content you share.
faq.cat3q2: Does Lina share data with third parties?
faq.cat3a2: No. Lina does not sell or share user data with third parties. There is no advertising in the app.
faq.cat3q3: Can messages be deleted or edited?
faq.cat3a3: No, messages in Lina cannot be edited or deleted after they are sent. This provides a clear and reliable record of what has been agreed between parents.
faq.cat3a4: You own your own data. Lina processes data only to deliver the service, and never for commercial purposes.
faq.cat3q5: What happens to my data if I delete my account?
faq.cat3a5: When the account is deleted, all personal information associated with you is removed. Messages, photos, and lists shared with the other parent remain available to them.
faq.cat4q1: How do I create an account?
faq.cat4a1: Download Lina from the App Store or Google Play, and follow the in-app guide to create an account. You only need your email address to sign in — no password is stored.
faq.cat4q2: Can I have multiple children in the app?
faq.cat4a2: Yes, you can add multiple children to Lina. Each child has their own shared space with associated information, messages, and photos.
faq.cat4q3: How do I invite the other parent?
faq.cat4a3: You share the invitation code via the app.
faq.cat5q1: What should a care agreement include?
faq.cat5a1: A good care agreement should cover the care pattern, place of residence, pick-up and drop-off, holiday and public holiday arrangements, cost sharing, communication between parents, travel with the child, and routines for changes and review of the agreement.
faq.cat5q2: Is a care agreement legally binding?
faq.cat5a2: A private care agreement is based on mutual agreement between the parents. It is not legally binding in the same way as a court order, but provides structure and predictability for both parties and the child.
faq.cat5q3: Is there a free tool for making a care agreement?
faq.cat5a3: In Norway, Bufdir offers a free digital parenting agreement where parents fill out the agreement step by step and sign with BankID. Similar official tools exist in Sweden, Denmark, and Finland.
faq.cat5q4: Does Lina help with care agreements?
faq.cat5a4: Lina offers an information page about what a care agreement should include, with an example and links to official resources in Norway, Sweden, Denmark, and Finland.

about.heading: About Lina
about.lead: Lina is a co-parenting app for parents who share care of a child across two homes. The same overview, the same information — regardless of which home the child is in.
about.whyText1: Lina was created because there was no co-parenting app that felt calm and focused. Most tools were built around conflict or legal processes. We wanted something different — a quiet, structured space for everyday coordination between two parents.
about.whyText2: The goal is to reduce the mental load that comes with shared care. When both parents have access to the same schedules, lists, and updates, no one has to carry everything alone.
about.focusText1: Lina is built around structured communication and practical tools. Threads keep conversations organized. Shared lists, contacts, and information about the child are always available — visible across both homes.
about.focusText2: There are no algorithms, no ads, and no social features. Lina is designed to be used when needed and to stay out of the way when it is not.

stories.label: Everyday scenarios
stories.title: How parents use Lina
stories.description: Small, everyday moments where having one shared place makes the difference. These are the situations Lina is built for.

s1.label: Child information
s1.title: The allergy the other home did not know about
s1.situation: A child is diagnosed with a new food allergy at a doctor's visit. The information needs to reach the other home before the next handover.
s1.resolution: With Lina: The parent updates the child's medical information once. The other parent sees it immediately — no need for a separate text or call.

s2.label: Equipment lists
s2.title: The rain jacket that is always at the wrong house
s2.situation: A child arrives at school without rain gear because no one was sure which home had it. Small things like this create unnecessary friction.
s2.resolution: With Lina: Shared equipment lists show what is at each home. Both parents can check and update the list, so the jacket follows the child.

s3.label: Threads
s3.title: The school event buried in a text chain
s3.situation: School sends information about a parents' meeting. One parent forwards it by text, but the details get lost between practical messages about pickup and dinner.
s3.resolution: With Lina: A dedicated thread for school keeps everything in one place. Both parents find what they need without scrolling through unrelated messages.

s4.label: Shared album
s4.title: The first bicycle ride you almost missed
s4.situation: A child learns to ride a bicycle at one home. The other parent only hears about it days later and never sees the moment.
s4.resolution: With Lina: Photos go into the shared album. Both parents can see the big and small moments, regardless of which home the child is in.

s5.label: Care schedule
s5.title: The handover mix-up on a holiday week
s5.situation: Holiday weeks change the regular schedule. Both parents remember a different version of the plan, and the child ends up in the middle.
s5.resolution: With Lina: The care schedule shows both parents the same overview. Changes are visible immediately, so there is one version of the truth.

s6.label: Contacts
s6.title: The dentist number no one could find
s6.situation: A child has a toothache at one home. The parent needs to call the dentist but does not have the number — it was saved in the other parent's phone.
s6.resolution: With Lina: Shared contacts mean both parents have the dentist, doctor, and school numbers available at all times.

stories.closing: These are not dramatic problems. They are the quiet, everyday moments where having one shared place saves time, reduces stress, and keeps the focus on the child.

termsSummary.heading: Terms summary
termsSummary.disclaimer: This summary is provided for convenience only. In the event of any inconsistency, the English version of the Terms of Service above shall prevail.
termsSummary.p1: Lina is a private cooperation tool for two parents who share responsibility for a child.
termsSummary.p2: You must be at least 18 years old and a parent or legal guardian to use the service. Each Lina space is intended for two connected parents.
termsSummary.p3: You keep ownership of all content you share in Lina. Lina only processes content to provide the service and does not sell or publicly share it.
termsSummary.p4: Lina is offered as a paid subscription. Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled through your platform account settings.
termsSummary.p5: Lina is a support tool and does not provide legal, medical, or professional advice, nor does it mediate disputes between parents.
termsSummary.p6: Shared content may remain available to the other parent after account deletion.
termsSummary.p7: For full details, see the Terms of Service.

privacySummary.heading: Privacy summary
privacySummary.disclaimer: This summary is provided for convenience only. In the event of any inconsistency, the English version of the Privacy Policy above shall prevail.
privacySummary.p1: Lina is built with privacy as a core principle.
privacySummary.p2: We process only personal data that is necessary to provide the service and support cooperation between parents. We do not use advertising, tracking, or marketing cookies.
privacySummary.p3: Only strictly necessary local storage is used to remember essential settings, such as Care Schedule preferences. The website uses Cloudflare Web Analytics for anonymous page view statistics — no cookies, no personal data, no cross-site tracking.
privacySummary.p4: When an account is deleted, personal account data is removed. Shared content may remain available to the other parent, as it may be jointly used within the service.
privacySummary.p5: All child-related data is provided and managed by parents or legal guardians.
privacySummary.p6: No personal data is sold or shared for commercial purposes.
privacySummary.p7: For full details, please refer to this Privacy Policy.

howTo.title: How to delete your account
howTo.intro: Users can request deletion of their Lina account in one of the following ways.
howTo.emailBefore: If you cannot access the app, contact us at:
howTo.emailAfter: — please include the email address associated with your Lina account.

whatHappens.title: What happens when you delete your account
whatHappens.item1: Your personal account information (such as email address and profile data) is removed
whatHappens.item2: Your access to Lina is permanently disabled
whatHappens.sharedContent: Shared content (such as messages, photos, and lists) is not automatically deleted, as it may be shared with another parent. This data remains available to the other connected parent.
whatHappens.retention: Some information may be retained temporarily where required by law or for security purposes, after which it is deleted or anonymised.

s2.title: The rain jacket that is always at the wrong house
s2.description: Shared equipment lists show what is at each home. Updates are visible right away, so the jacket follows the child.

s3.title: The first bicycle ride you almost missed
s3.description: Photos go into the shared album. The big and small moments are shared automatically — regardless of which home the child is in.
